Info: Worked alot with Procedural world generation using many of the Unreal Engines Procedural Content Generation Pipeline, mainly using Procedural Constent Graph
This was my first time in the professional game development industry and seing how things operate there was interesting and made me learn alot.

It was an honor to work with all the amazing people at Invisible Walls and their amazing games like one of my favorites being
Neighbors: Suburban Warfare

Studied for Two Years with an additional 8 Months of internship. Under the two years at TGA I collaborated on 7 different game projects with different generes and styles in a specified time range. We were usually 10 - 15 other students in different diciplines working on all these projects.
TGA is where most of my C++ knowledge had been aquired but have also done many of my own projects written in C++

I spent three years studying Game Programming specificaly learning C# and using it with Unity. We also learned how to use different software like Maya, Photoshop And Audacity, The basics of them anyway
